scenario: A process notifies a mobile-app push subscription topic via AWS SNS when an order ships. The team uses out-of-the-box Connectors.
question: Which Connector fits?

options.text: A generic REST Outbound Connector calling the SNS HTTP API directly would require manually implementing AWS Signature Version 4 request signing, constructing the correct request headers and body, and handling SNS-specific error codes. This re-implements authentication and protocol handling that the dedicated AWS SNS Outbound Connector already provides natively. Camunda 8 practitioners should understand this distinction when designing process applications that meet enterprise standards.

AWS SNS Outbound Connector -- configure the topic ARN, AWS credentials via Connector Secrets (accessKey, secretKey, region), and message payload. The Connector handles AWS Signature V4 authentication, payload composition, and SNS-specific error responses out of the box without any custom code. It is a first-class out-of-the-box Connector in the Camunda library. 0 in Camunda 8.

Building a custom Java Connector using the Connector SDK would require implementing the entire SNS integration from scratch. Understanding this characteristic is essential for the Camunda 8 Certified Professional examination.
The AWS SNS Outbound Connector is available in both SaaS and Self-Managed deployments. Teams building on Camunda 8 should consider this when designing process models for production deployment.

a.text: Incorrect. AWS SNS Outbound Connector is a first-class out-of-the-box Connector. Handles AWS auth (signature v4), payload composition, error responses.

b.text: Correct. REST manually requires signing AWS requests -- re-implementing what the SNS Connector handles.

explanation: REST manually requires signing AWS requests -- re-implementing what the SNS Connector handles.
